trans-unit.source: The Global Aggregation of Indicators for Anticipatory Action (GAIA) Pipeline produces a series of thematic files for a collection from several countries at administrative level 2, covering multiple countries where administrative boundaries and place codes (PCODEs) are available on HDX. Each file captures different aspects of population, infrastructure, and environmental conditions. Below is an overview of the available files:
trans-unit.target: The Global Aggregation of Indicators for Anticipatory Action (GAIA) Pipeline produces a series of thematic files for a collection from several countries at administrative level 2, covering multiple countries where administrative boundaries and place codes (PCODEs) are available on HDX. Each file captures different aspects of population, infrastructure, and environmental conditions. Below is an overview of the available files:

trans-unit.source: [**Access to Services**](#access-to-services) – Population accessibility to key facilities such as education and health centers.
trans-unit.target: [**Access to Services**](#access-to-services) – Population accessibility to key facilities such as education and health centers.

trans-unit.source: [**Facilities**](#facilities) – Availability and distribution of essential service infrastructure.
trans-unit.target: [**Facilities**](#facilities) – Availability and distribution of essential service infrastructure.

trans-unit.source: [**Coping Capacity**](#coping-capacity) – Combined indicators derived from Access, Facilities, Evacuability, and RAI layers.
trans-unit.target: [**Coping Capacity**](#coping-capacity) – Combined indicators derived from Access, Facilities, Evacuability, and RAI layers.

trans-unit.source: [**Rural Population**](#rural-population) – Demographic indicators focused specifically on rural populations.
trans-unit.target: [**Rural Population**](#rural-population) – Demographic indicators focused specifically on rural populations.

trans-unit.source: [**Rural Accessibility Index (RAI)**](#rural-accessibility-index-rai) – Percentage of rural population within 2 km of a paved road.
trans-unit.target: [**Rural Accessibility Index (RAI)**](#rural-accessibility-index-rai) – Percentage of rural population within 2 km of a paved road.

trans-unit.source: [**Vulnerability**](#vulnerability) – Composite indicators derived from Demographics and Rural Population layers.
trans-unit.target: [**Vulnerability**](#vulnerability) – Composite indicators derived from Demographics and Rural Population layers.

trans-unit.source: [**Flood Exposure**](#flood-exposure) – Exposure of populations and facilities to flood hazards.
trans-unit.target: [**Flood Exposure**](#flood-exposure) – Exposure of populations and facilities to flood hazards.

trans-unit.source: [**Cyclone Exposure**](#cyclone-exposure) – Exposure of populations and facilities to cyclone hazards.
trans-unit.target: [**Cyclone Exposure**](#cyclone-exposure) – Exposure of populations and facilities to cyclone hazards.

trans-unit.source: [**Evacuability**](#evacuability) – Travel time from at-risk areas to the nearest safe zone.
trans-unit.target: [**Evacuability**](#evacuability) – Travel time from at-risk areas to the nearest safe zone.

trans-unit.source: Further details on GAIA’s methodology are available in the [GAIA repository on GitHub](https://github.com/GIScience/gaia).
trans-unit.target: Further details on GAIA’s methodology are available in the [GAIA repository on GitHub](https://github.com/GIScience/gaia).

trans-unit.source: :::{admonition} Information :class: tip The **Coping Capacity**, **Vulnerability**, and **Flood Exposure** files can be used directly as input for the Risk Assessment QGIS Plugin when analyzing flood hazards. Only minor adjustments, such as renaming the ID columns, are required for compatibility. :::
trans-unit.target: :::{admonition} Information :class: tip The **Coping Capacity**, **Vulnerability**, and **Flood Exposure** files can be used directly as input for the Risk Assessment QGIS Plugin when analyzing flood hazards. Only minor adjustments, such as renaming the ID columns, are required for compatibility. :::

trans-unit.source: Represents the share of the population with access to key facilities within defined distances or travel times.
trans-unit.target: Represents the share of the population with access to key facilities within defined distances or travel times.

trans-unit.source: Accessibility data (isochrones) downloaded from **MinIO** for the categories: `education`, `hospitals`, `primary_healthcare`.
trans-unit.target: Accessibility data (isochrones) downloaded from **MinIO** for the categories: `education`, `hospitals`, `primary_healthcare`.

trans-unit.source: Population data: WorldPop raster data (vulnerable populations) via *Demographics*.
trans-unit.target: Population data: WorldPop raster data (vulnerable populations) via *Demographics*.

trans-unit.source: **Download accessibility isochrones** in GPKG format for each category.
trans-unit.target: **Download accessibility isochrones** in GPKG format for each category.

trans-unit.source: **Load administrative boundaries** at admin level 2 from local GeoJSON files.
trans-unit.target: **Load administrative boundaries** at admin level 2 from local GeoJSON files.

trans-unit.source: **Fetch WorldPop raster datasets** and sum relevant indicators to produce a vulnerable population raster.
trans-unit.target: **Fetch WorldPop raster datasets** and sum relevant indicators to produce a vulnerable population raster.

trans-unit.source: Select isochrone polygons for the cutoff value.
trans-unit.target: Select isochrone polygons for the cutoff value.

trans-unit.source: Use `rasterstats.zonal_stats` to compute total population within the isochrone area.
trans-unit.target: Use `rasterstats.zonal_stats` to compute total population within the isochrone area.

trans-unit.source: Save results as a CSV containing population counts per administrative unit.
trans-unit.target: Save results as a CSV containing population counts per administrative unit.

trans-unit.source: Represents the availability and spatial distribution of essential service infrastructure such as schools, hospitals, and primary healthcare centers.
trans-unit.target: Represents the availability and spatial distribution of essential service infrastructure such as schools, hospitals, and primary healthcare centers.

trans-unit.source: **Overpass API**, for direct querying of recent OSM feature data; or
trans-unit.target: **Overpass API**, for direct querying of recent OSM feature data; or

trans-unit.source: **Ohsome API**, for more stable and filtered access to OSM geometries.
trans-unit.target: **Ohsome API**, for more stable and filtered access to OSM geometries.

trans-unit.source: **Administrative boundaries** (GeoJSON): Used to spatially aggregate features at administrative level 2.
trans-unit.target: **Administrative boundaries** (GeoJSON): Used to spatially aggregate features at administrative level 2.

trans-unit.source: **Load administrative boundaries** (GeoJSON) for the target country at the desired administrative level.
trans-unit.target: **Load administrative boundaries** (GeoJSON) for the target country at the desired administrative level.

trans-unit.source: **Primary healthcare:** Non-hospital facilities providing healthcare services, such as `clinic`, `doctors`, `midwife`, `nurse`, or `center`.
trans-unit.target: **Primary healthcare:** Non-hospital facilities providing healthcare services, such as `clinic`, `doctors`, `midwife`, `nurse`, or `center`.

trans-unit.source: If Overpass is used, queries are dynamically built and executed using bounding boxes of the country boundary.
trans-unit.target: If Overpass is used, queries are dynamically built and executed using bounding boxes of the country boundary.

trans-unit.source: If Ohsome is used, geometry data is fetched for the same bounding box and time period (if specified).
trans-unit.target: If Ohsome is used, geometry data is fetched for the same bounding box and time period (if specified).

trans-unit.source: For Overpass: CSV results are parsed into GeoDataFrames with `lon`, `lat`, and `osmId` attributes.
trans-unit.target: For Overpass: CSV results are parsed into GeoDataFrames with `lon`, `lat`, and `osmId` attributes.

trans-unit.source: For Ohsome: JSON features are directly converted into GeoDataFrames.
trans-unit.target: For Ohsome: JSON features are directly converted into GeoDataFrames.

trans-unit.source: Each facility type (education, hospitals, primary healthcare) is stored as a separate GeoJSON in a `Temporary` folder.
trans-unit.target: Each facility type (education, hospitals, primary healthcare) is stored as a separate GeoJSON in a `Temporary` folder.

trans-unit.source: Facilities are spatially joined (`sjoin`) with administrative polygons.
trans-unit.target: Facilities are spatially joined (`sjoin`) with administrative polygons.

trans-unit.source: For each category, counts are grouped by `{admin_level}_PCODE`.
trans-unit.target: For each category, counts are grouped by `{admin_level}_PCODE`.

trans-unit.source: Counts for all categories are merged into a single summary table.
trans-unit.target: Counts for all categories are merged into a single summary table.

trans-unit.source: Output is saved as a CSV file in the `Output` directory.
trans-unit.target: Output is saved as a CSV file in the `Output` directory.

trans-unit.source: Units: number of facilities per administrative unit
trans-unit.target: Units: number of facilities per administrative unit

trans-unit.source: Stored in `Temporary/` folder as raw point data for each facility category.
trans-unit.target: Stored in `Temporary/` folder as raw point data for each facility category.

trans-unit.source: Represents the ability of a population to access essential services and benefit from available infrastructure. This layer combines *Access to Services*, *Facilities*, *Evacuability*, and *Rural Accessibility Index (RAI)* indicators to assess local capacity to cope with shocks or disruptions.
trans-unit.target: Represents the ability of a population to access essential services and benefit from available infrastructure. This layer combines *Access to Services*, *Facilities*, *Evacuability*, and *Rural Accessibility Index (RAI)* indicators to assess local capacity to cope with shocks or disruptions.

trans-unit.source: **Access to Services CSVs:** Derived from population accessibility analysis (see *Access to Services* chapter).
trans-unit.target: **Access to Services CSVs:** Derived from population accessibility analysis (see *Access to Services* chapter).

trans-unit.source: **Facilities CSVs:** Derived from OpenStreetMap facility data (see *Facilities* chapter).
trans-unit.target: **Facilities CSVs:** Derived from OpenStreetMap facility data (see *Facilities* chapter).

trans-unit.source: **Evacuability CSVs:** Travel time from at-risk population areas to nearest safe zones (see *Evacuability* chapter).
trans-unit.target: **Evacuability CSVs:** Travel time from at-risk population areas to nearest safe zones (see *Evacuability* chapter).

trans-unit.source: All inputs are aggregated at the same administrative level (e.g., ADM2).
trans-unit.target: All inputs are aggregated at the same administrative level (e.g., ADM2).

trans-unit.source: *Access to Services*, *Facilities*, *Evacuability*, and *RAI* CSVs are provided per administrative level.
trans-unit.target: *Access to Services*, *Facilities*, *Evacuability*, and *RAI* CSVs are provided per administrative level.

trans-unit.source: Each CSV includes an identifier column such as `ADM0_PCODE`, `ADM1_PCODE`, or `ADM2_PCODE`.
trans-unit.target: Each CSV includes an identifier column such as `ADM0_PCODE`, `ADM1_PCODE`, or `ADM2_PCODE`.

trans-unit.source: The script automatically detects the administrative identifier column by searching for any column ending with `_PCODE`.
trans-unit.target: The script automatically detects the administrative identifier column by searching for any column ending with `_PCODE`.

trans-unit.source: The access and facilities datasets are merged on the identified `*_PCODE` column using a left join.
trans-unit.target: The access and facilities datasets are merged on the identified `*_PCODE` column using a left join.

trans-unit.source: If available, evacuability columns are merged from the evacuability CSV.
trans-unit.target: If available, evacuability columns are merged from the evacuability CSV.

trans-unit.source: If available, RAI columns are merged from the RAI CSV.
trans-unit.target: If available, RAI columns are merged from the RAI CSV.

trans-unit.source: The merged dataset is saved as `{country_code}_{admin_level}_coping.csv` under `Output/`.
trans-unit.target: The merged dataset is saved as `{country_code}_{admin_level}_coping.csv` under `Output/`.

trans-unit.source: One output is produced per administrative level present in the input data.
trans-unit.target: One output is produced per administrative level present in the input data.

trans-unit.source: Units: counts and population values aggregated per administrative unit
trans-unit.target: Units: counts and population values aggregated per administrative unit

trans-unit.source: Provides population distribution indicators based on **age** and **sex**. This layer is derived from the [**WorldPop**](https://www.worldpop.org/) global population dataset and quantifies vulnerable population groups across administrative boundaries.
trans-unit.target: Provides population distribution indicators based on **age** and **sex**. This layer is derived from the [**WorldPop**](https://www.worldpop.org/) global population dataset and quantifies vulnerable population groups across administrative boundaries.

trans-unit.source: **WorldPop Age-Sex Population Rasters:** Downloaded from the [WorldPop Global Constrained Population dataset](https://www.worldpop.org/geodata/listing?id=77) (`Global_2015_2030`, Release `R2025A`, Year `2030`).
trans-unit.target: **WorldPop Age-Sex Population Rasters:** Downloaded from the [WorldPop Global Constrained Population dataset](https://www.worldpop.org/geodata/listing?id=77) (`Global_2015_2030`, Release `R2025A`, Year `2030`).

trans-unit.source: **Administrative boundaries** (GeoJSON): Used to spatially aggregate features at administrative level 2.
trans-unit.target: **Administrative boundaries** (GeoJSON): Used to spatially aggregate features at administrative level 2.

trans-unit.source: Each demographic indicator represents the **sum of population counts** matching specified age and sex ranges:
trans-unit.target: Each demographic indicator represents the **sum of population counts** matching specified age and sex ranges:

trans-unit.source: For each indicator, the script identifies which age/sex rasters are needed (e.g., `m_00`, `f_01`, etc.).
trans-unit.target: For each indicator, the script identifies which age/sex rasters are needed (e.g., `m_00`, `f_01`, etc.).

trans-unit.source: Tiles are downloaded from **Worldpop** using the `Global_2015_2030` / `R2025A` / `2030` constrained dataset.
trans-unit.target: Tiles are downloaded from **Worldpop** using the `Global_2015_2030` / `R2025A` / `2030` constrained dataset.

trans-unit.source: The corresponding age/sex tiles are summed into one raster per indicator.
trans-unit.target: The corresponding age/sex tiles are summed into one raster per indicator.

trans-unit.source: Using the administrative boundary GeoJSON (e.g., `ADM2`), zonal statistics are computed for each indicator.
trans-unit.target: Using the administrative boundary GeoJSON (e.g., `ADM2`), zonal statistics are computed for each indicator.

trans-unit.source: Population counts are summed per administrative area.
trans-unit.target: Population counts are summed per administrative area.

trans-unit.source: `dependency_ratio = (dep_dependents / dep_working) × 100` is computed per administrative unit.
trans-unit.target: `dependency_ratio = (dep_dependents / dep_working) × 100` is computed per administrative unit.

trans-unit.source: **Units:** Population counts (integer, aggregated per administrative unit), ratio (%)
trans-unit.target: **Units:** Population counts (integer, aggregated per administrative unit), ratio (%)

trans-unit.source: Represents the proportion and distribution of people living in **rural areas** within each administrative unit. Rural areas are those outside urban extents, typically characterized by lower population density, agricultural or natural land use, and limited infrastructure compared to urban centers.
trans-unit.target: Represents the proportion and distribution of people living in **rural areas** within each administrative unit. Rural areas are those outside urban extents, typically characterized by lower population density, agricultural or natural land use, and limited infrastructure compared to urban centers.

trans-unit.source: This layer combines **WorldPop population rasters** with **Global Human Settlement Layer (GHS-SMOD)** data to estimate rural populations for each demographic group.
trans-unit.target: This layer combines **WorldPop population rasters** with **Global Human Settlement Layer (GHS-SMOD)** data to estimate rural populations for each demographic group.

trans-unit.source: **WorldPop Population Rasters:** Derived from the [WorldPop Age-Sex structured datasets](https://www.worldpop.org/geodata/listing?id=77). Used to calculate population counts for various demographic indicators (see the *Demographics* chapter).
trans-unit.target: **WorldPop Population Rasters:** Derived from the [WorldPop Age-Sex structured datasets](https://www.worldpop.org/geodata/listing?id=77). Used to calculate population counts for various demographic indicators (see the *Demographics* chapter).

trans-unit.source: **GHS-SMOD (Settlement Model) Raster:** Downloaded from the [JRC GHSL repository](https://jeodpp.jrc.ec.europa.eu/ftp/jrc-opendata/GHSL/) for the year 2030 projection. Used to classify grid cells as **urban** or **rural** based on settlement patterns.
trans-unit.target: **GHS-SMOD (Settlement Model) Raster:** Downloaded from the [JRC GHSL repository](https://jeodpp.jrc.ec.europa.eu/ftp/jrc-opendata/GHSL/) for the year 2030 projection. Used to classify grid cells as **urban** or **rural** based on settlement patterns.

trans-unit.source: **Administrative Boundaries:** GeoJSON boundary files (e.g., ADM2) define the spatial units used for population aggregation.
trans-unit.target: **Administrative Boundaries:** GeoJSON boundary files (e.g., ADM2) define the spatial units used for population aggregation.

trans-unit.source: The script downloads the global SMOD ZIP archive for 2030.
trans-unit.target: The script downloads the global SMOD ZIP archive for 2030.

trans-unit.source: Extracts and selects the `.tif` raster corresponding to global settlement data.
trans-unit.target: Extracts and selects the `.tif` raster corresponding to global settlement data.

trans-unit.source: Converts original SMOD categories into two classes:
trans-unit.target: Converts original SMOD categories into two classes:

trans-unit.source: The resulting raster is saved as `smod_reclass.tif`.
trans-unit.target: The resulting raster is saved as `smod_reclass.tif`.

trans-unit.source: Ensures that population rasters for indicators such as `female_pop`, `children_u5`, etc., are available.
trans-unit.target: Ensures that population rasters for indicators such as `female_pop`, `children_u5`, etc., are available.

trans-unit.source: Reprojects the SMOD raster to match each WorldPop raster.
trans-unit.target: Reprojects the SMOD raster to match each WorldPop raster.

trans-unit.source: Multiplies each population raster by the rural mask (`smod == 1`).
trans-unit.target: Multiplies each population raster by the rural mask (`smod == 1`).

trans-unit.source: Performs zonal statistics per administrative unit (e.g., ADM2).
trans-unit.target: Performs zonal statistics per administrative unit (e.g., ADM2).

trans-unit.source: Aggregates results into a CSV file per country and administrative level.
trans-unit.target: Aggregates results into a CSV file per country and administrative level.

trans-unit.source: Adds both absolute (`_rural`) and relative (`_rural_perc`) population values.
trans-unit.target: Adds both absolute (`_rural`) and relative (`_rural_perc`) population values.

trans-unit.source: `rural_pop_perc` (percentage of population living in rural areas)
trans-unit.target: `rural_pop_perc` (percentage of population living in rural areas)

trans-unit.source: Units: counts and percentages per administrative unit
trans-unit.target: Units: counts and percentages per administrative unit

trans-unit.source: Computes the percentage of rural population with access to a paved road within 2 km. The Rural Accessibility Index measures the proportion of people living in rural areas (GHS-SMOD classes 11–13) who live within 2 km of a paved road surface. Results are provided for multiple demographic groups and as a dependency ratio for the accessible rural population.
trans-unit.target: Computes the percentage of rural population with access to a paved road within 2 km. The Rural Accessibility Index measures the proportion of people living in rural areas (GHS-SMOD classes 11–13) who live within 2 km of a paved road surface. Results are provided for multiple demographic groups and as a dependency ratio for the accessible rural population.

trans-unit.source: **Road Surface Data:** Mapillary road surface classification (`pred_label == 0` for paved) and Planet road surface classification (`DL_road_class_2024 == "paved"`), downloaded from HEiGIT/HDX storage.
trans-unit.target: **Road Surface Data:** Mapillary road surface classification (`pred_label == 0` for paved) and Planet road surface classification (`DL_road_class_2024 == "paved"`), downloaded from HEiGIT/HDX storage.

trans-unit.source: **GHS-SMOD Raster:** Global Human Settlement Layer settlement model for 2030, used to classify grid cells as rural (classes 11–13).
trans-unit.target: **GHS-SMOD Raster:** Global Human Settlement Layer settlement model for 2030, used to classify grid cells as rural (classes 11–13).

trans-unit.source: **WorldPop Population Rasters:** Used to extract population counts per demographic group within accessible rural areas.
trans-unit.target: **WorldPop Population Rasters:** Used to extract population counts per demographic group within accessible rural areas.

trans-unit.source: **Administrative Boundaries** (GeoJSON): Used to disaggregate results per admin unit.
trans-unit.target: **Administrative Boundaries** (GeoJSON): Used to disaggregate results per admin unit.

trans-unit.source: Population in rural areas within 2 km of a paved road
trans-unit.target: Population in rural areas within 2 km of a paved road

trans-unit.source: Rural Accessibility Index = rural access / rural pop × 100
trans-unit.target: Rural Accessibility Index = rural access / rural pop × 100

trans-unit.source: Dependency ratio within accessible rural areas
trans-unit.target: Dependency ratio within accessible rural areas

trans-unit.source: **Download road surface data** (Mapillary and Planet) for the country, filter to paved roads only.
trans-unit.target: **Download road surface data** (Mapillary and Planet) for the country, filter to paved roads only.

trans-unit.source: **Buffer paved roads by 2 km** in a projected CRS (UTM zone estimated from country centroid).
trans-unit.target: **Buffer paved roads by 2 km** in a projected CRS (UTM zone estimated from country centroid).

trans-unit.source: **Intersect buffered roads with admin boundaries** to determine per-admin-unit road service areas.
trans-unit.target: **Intersect buffered roads with admin boundaries** to determine per-admin-unit road service areas.

trans-unit.source: **Download and reclassify GHS-SMOD raster** into rural (1) / urban (2) / excluded (0).
trans-unit.target: **Download and reclassify GHS-SMOD raster** into rural (1) / urban (2) / excluded (0).

trans-unit.source: **Clip SMOD to country boundary** and vectorize rural pixels into polygons.
trans-unit.target: **Clip SMOD to country boundary** and vectorize rural pixels into polygons.

trans-unit.source: **Intersect rural polygons with buffered roads** to identify accessible rural areas.
trans-unit.target: **Intersect rural polygons with buffered roads** to identify accessible rural areas.

trans-unit.source: **Extract WorldPop population counts** for each demographic group within accessible rural areas.
trans-unit.target: **Extract WorldPop population counts** for each demographic group within accessible rural areas.

trans-unit.source: **Load rural population totals** from the *Rural Population* CSV to use as denominator.
trans-unit.target: **Load rural population totals** from the *Rural Population* CSV to use as denominator.

trans-unit.source: Represents the sensitivity of a population to external shocks based on demographic composition and settlement type. This layer combines *Demographics* and *Rural Population* indicators to highlight population groups more likely to experience heightened vulnerability in rural or hard-to-reach regions.
trans-unit.target: Represents the sensitivity of a population to external shocks based on demographic composition and settlement type. This layer combines *Demographics* and *Rural Population* indicators to highlight population groups more likely to experience heightened vulnerability in rural or hard-to-reach regions.

trans-unit.source: **Demographics CSVs:** Derived from [WorldPop](https://www.worldpop.org/) population datasets, disaggregated by age and sex (see *Demographics* chapter).
trans-unit.target: **Demographics CSVs:** Derived from [WorldPop](https://www.worldpop.org/) population datasets, disaggregated by age and sex (see *Demographics* chapter).

trans-unit.source: **Rural Population CSVs:** Derived from settlement layer analysis separating rural and urban populations (see *Rural Population* chapter).
trans-unit.target: **Rural Population CSVs:** Derived from settlement layer analysis separating rural and urban populations (see *Rural Population* chapter).

trans-unit.source: Both datasets are aggregated to the same administrative level (e.g., ADM2).
trans-unit.target: Both datasets are aggregated to the same administrative level (e.g., ADM2).

trans-unit.source: One *Demographics* CSV and one *Rural Population* CSV are provided per administrative level.
trans-unit.target: One *Demographics* CSV and one *Rural Population* CSV are provided per administrative level.

trans-unit.source: Each file contains an identifier column such as `ADM0_PCODE`, `ADM1_PCODE`, or `ADM2_PCODE`.
trans-unit.target: Each file contains an identifier column such as `ADM0_PCODE`, `ADM1_PCODE`, or `ADM2_PCODE`.

trans-unit.source: The script automatically detects the administrative identifier column by finding any column ending with `_PCODE`.
trans-unit.target: The script automatically detects the administrative identifier column by finding any column ending with `_PCODE`.

trans-unit.source: The two datasets are merged on the identified `*_PCODE` column using a **left join**.
trans-unit.target: The two datasets are merged on the identified `*_PCODE` column using a **left join**.

trans-unit.source: This ensures demographic indicators are retained even when some administrative areas lack rural population data.
trans-unit.target: This ensures demographic indicators are retained even when some administrative areas lack rural population data.

trans-unit.source: The merged dataset is exported as `{country_code}_{admin_level}_vulnerability.csv` to the `Output/` directory.
trans-unit.target: The merged dataset is exported as `{country_code}_{admin_level}_vulnerability.csv` to the `Output/` directory.

trans-unit.source: One file is created per administrative level present in the input data.
trans-unit.target: One file is created per administrative level present in the input data.

trans-unit.source: **Units:** Population counts and ratios aggregated per administrative unit
trans-unit.target: **Units:** Population counts and ratios aggregated per administrative unit

trans-unit.source: Estimates population and facility exposure to flooding using **GLOFAS flood hazard rasters** and **WorldPop demographic layers**. Flooded areas are identified by usign a threshold of 30 cm, and exposure metrics are aggregated per administrative boundary.
trans-unit.target: Estimates population and facility exposure to flooding using **GLOFAS flood hazard rasters** and **WorldPop demographic layers**. Flooded areas are identified by usign a threshold of 30 cm, and exposure metrics are aggregated per administrative boundary.

trans-unit.source: This layer provides indicators for the number of people affected per demographic group and the percentage/count of critical facilities (education, hospitals, primary healthcare) impacted by flood events of different return periods (RPs).
trans-unit.target: This layer provides indicators for the number of people affected per demographic group and the percentage/count of critical facilities (education, hospitals, primary healthcare) impacted by flood events of different return periods (RPs).

trans-unit.source: **GLOFAS Flood Hazard Rasters** (`https://jeodpp.jrc.ec.europa.eu/ftp/jrc-opendata/CEMS-GLOFAS/flood_hazard/`) Raster tiles containing flood depth values for different return periods (RPs).
trans-unit.target: **GLOFAS Flood Hazard Rasters** (`https://jeodpp.jrc.ec.europa.eu/ftp/jrc-opendata/CEMS-GLOFAS/flood_hazard/`) Raster tiles containing flood depth values for different return periods (RPs).

trans-unit.source: **WorldPop Population Layers** (`scripts/fetch_worldpop.py`) Demographic rasters for multiple indicators at ~100 m resolution: total population, female, children under 5, elderly, etc.
trans-unit.target: **WorldPop Population Layers** (`scripts/fetch_worldpop.py`) Demographic rasters for multiple indicators at ~100 m resolution: total population, female, children under 5, elderly, etc.

trans-unit.source: **Facilities Data** (`scripts/fetch_facilities_ohsome_overpass.py`) Point geometries of critical infrastructure from OHSOME or Overpass APIs.
trans-unit.target: **Facilities Data** (`scripts/fetch_facilities_ohsome_overpass.py`) Point geometries of critical infrastructure from OHSOME or Overpass APIs.

trans-unit.source: **Administrative Boundaries** (GeoJSON) Used to aggregate flood exposure per ADM level (e.g., ADM1).
trans-unit.target: **Administrative Boundaries** (GeoJSON) Used to aggregate flood exposure per ADM level (e.g., ADM1).

trans-unit.source: Number of females affected by flood depth ≥ threshold
trans-unit.target: Number of females affected by flood depth ≥ threshold

trans-unit.source: Number of female children under 5 affected
trans-unit.target: Number of female children under 5 affected

trans-unit.source: Number of female population under 15 affected
trans-unit.target: Number of female population under 15 affected

trans-unit.source: Number of women of reproductive age (15–49) affected
trans-unit.target: Number of women of reproductive age (15–49) affected

trans-unit.source: Dependency ratio of the affected population
trans-unit.target: Dependency ratio of the affected population

trans-unit.source: Percentage of primary healthcare facilities flooded
trans-unit.target: Percentage of primary healthcare facilities flooded

trans-unit.source: Number of primary healthcare facilities flooded
trans-unit.target: Number of primary healthcare facilities flooded

trans-unit.source: `{rp}` is the return period (e.g., 10, 50, 100 years), `{thresh}` is the flood threshold in cm.
trans-unit.target: `{rp}` is the return period (e.g., 10, 50, 100 years), `{thresh}` is the flood threshold in cm.

trans-unit.source: Minimum flood depth considered for exposure (configurable in `assets_config.yaml`)
trans-unit.target: Minimum flood depth considered for exposure (configurable in `assets_config.yaml`)

trans-unit.source: Computes `THRESH_SUFFIX` (flood depth in cm) for indicator naming.
trans-unit.target: Computes `THRESH_SUFFIX` (flood depth in cm) for indicator naming.

trans-unit.source: Queries GLOFAS server for all flood tiles corresponding to each RP.
trans-unit.target: Queries GLOFAS server for all flood tiles corresponding to each RP.

trans-unit.source: Selects only tiles intersecting the country boundary.
trans-unit.target: Selects only tiles intersecting the country boundary.

trans-unit.source: Merges selected tiles into a single raster per RP.
trans-unit.target: Merges selected tiles into a single raster per RP.

trans-unit.source: Clips the merged raster to the country boundary and saves to `Temporary`.
trans-unit.target: Clips the merged raster to the country boundary and saves to `Temporary`.

trans-unit.source: Fetches required demographic rasters (female, children under 5, female under 5, elderly, under 15, female under 15, women of reproductive age, dependents, working-age population).
trans-unit.target: Fetches required demographic rasters (female, children under 5, female under 5, elderly, under 15, female under 15, women of reproductive age, dependents, working-age population).

trans-unit.source: Ensures rasters exist locally for alignment with flood data.
trans-unit.target: Ensures rasters exist locally for alignment with flood data.

trans-unit.source: Downloads or loads facilities geometries from the chosen API (`ohsome-api`, `overpass`).
trans-unit.target: Downloads or loads facilities geometries from the chosen API (`ohsome-api`, `overpass`).

trans-unit.source: Converts geometries to points and reprojects to match raster CRS.
trans-unit.target: Converts geometries to points and reprojects to match raster CRS.

trans-unit.source: **Flooded population**: Multiplies population raster by flood mask (flood depth ≥ threshold) to compute the number of affected people per ADM polygon.
trans-unit.target: **Flooded population**: Multiplies population raster by flood mask (flood depth ≥ threshold) to compute the number of affected people per ADM polygon.

trans-unit.source: **Flooded facilities**: Samples flood raster at facility locations to compute flooded count and percentage per ADM polygon.
trans-unit.target: **Flooded facilities**: Samples flood raster at facility locations to compute flooded count and percentage per ADM polygon.

trans-unit.source: Merges RP results across population and facilities into a single CSV.
trans-unit.target: Merges RP results across population and facilities into a single CSV.

trans-unit.source: Fills missing values with 0 and rounds numbers to integers.
trans-unit.target: Fills missing values with 0 and rounds numbers to integers.

trans-unit.source: Estimates the exposure of population and critical facilities to tropical cyclones using **IBTrACS** storm track data. Cyclone categories are based on the Saffir-Simpson scale and grouped into three exposure classes (Cat 1, Cat 2-3, Cat 4-5). For each administrative unit, the affected population per demographic group and the count/percentage of flooded facilities are computed per category.
trans-unit.target: Estimates the exposure of population and critical facilities to tropical cyclones using **IBTrACS** storm track data. Cyclone categories are based on the Saffir-Simpson scale and grouped into three exposure classes (Cat 1, Cat 2-3, Cat 4-5). For each administrative unit, the affected population per demographic group and the count/percentage of flooded facilities are computed per category.

trans-unit.source: **IBTrACS Storm Tracks:** International Best Track Archive for Climate Stewardship (since 1980), downloaded from NOAA NCEI as shapefile lines with Saffir-Simpson wind speed categories.
trans-unit.target: **IBTrACS Storm Tracks:** International Best Track Archive for Climate Stewardship (since 1980), downloaded from NOAA NCEI as shapefile lines with Saffir-Simpson wind speed categories.

trans-unit.source: **WorldPop Population Rasters:** Demographic rasters for multiple indicators used to compute exposed populations (see *Demographics* chapter).
trans-unit.target: **WorldPop Population Rasters:** Demographic rasters for multiple indicators used to compute exposed populations (see *Demographics* chapter).

trans-unit.source: **Facilities Data:** OSM-derived facility point geometries (education, hospitals, primary healthcare) from Ohsome or Overpass API (see *Facilities* chapter).
trans-unit.target: **Facilities Data:** OSM-derived facility point geometries (education, hospitals, primary healthcare) from Ohsome or Overpass API (see *Facilities* chapter).

trans-unit.source: **Administrative Boundaries** (GeoJSON): Used to aggregate exposure per ADM level.
trans-unit.target: **Administrative Boundaries** (GeoJSON): Used to aggregate exposure per ADM level.

trans-unit.source: Exposed population for a demographic indicator at cyclone category `{cls}`
trans-unit.target: Exposed population for a demographic indicator at cyclone category `{cls}`

trans-unit.source: Dependency ratio of the exposed population at category `{cls}`
trans-unit.target: Dependency ratio of the exposed population at category `{cls}`

trans-unit.source: Number of facilities exposed at category `{cls}`
trans-unit.target: Number of facilities exposed at category `{cls}`

trans-unit.source: Percentage of facilities exposed at category `{cls}`
trans-unit.target: Percentage of facilities exposed at category `{cls}`

trans-unit.source: Where `{cls}` is the cyclone exposure class (1 = Cat 1, 2 = Cat 2-3, 3 = Cat 4-5), `{indicator}` is a demographic group (`total_pop`, `female_pop`, `children_u5`, `female_u5`, `elderly`, `pop_u15`, `female_u15`, `wra_pop`), and `{facility}` is a category (`education`, `hospitals`, `primary_healthcare`).
trans-unit.target: Where `{cls}` is the cyclone exposure class (1 = Cat 1, 2 = Cat 2-3, 3 = Cat 4-5), `{indicator}` is a demographic group (`total_pop`, `female_pop`, `children_u5`, `female_u5`, `elderly`, `pop_u15`, `female_u15`, `wra_pop`), and `{facility}` is a category (`education`, `hospitals`, `primary_healthcare`).

trans-unit.source: **Download and extract IBTrACS storm track data** from NOAA NCEI.
trans-unit.target: **Download and extract IBTrACS storm track data** from NOAA NCEI.

trans-unit.source: **Filter storms** with Saffir-Simpson intensity ≥ 1 and intersecting the country bounding box.
trans-unit.target: **Filter storms** with Saffir-Simpson intensity ≥ 1 and intersecting the country bounding box.

trans-unit.source: **Buffer storm tracks** using the mean radius of maximum winds (R34) converted to meters.
trans-unit.target: **Buffer storm tracks** using the mean radius of maximum winds (R34) converted to meters.

trans-unit.source: **Clip buffers** to the country administrative boundary.
trans-unit.target: **Clip buffers** to the country administrative boundary.

trans-unit.source: **Rasterize** the buffered tracks into a classified exposure raster (classes 1, 2, 3) aligned to WorldPop resolution.
trans-unit.target: **Rasterize** the buffered tracks into a classified exposure raster (classes 1, 2, 3) aligned to WorldPop resolution.

trans-unit.source: **Compute population exposure:** For each demographic indicator and cyclone class, multiply the population raster by the class mask and sum per admin unit using zonal statistics.
trans-unit.target: **Compute population exposure:** For each demographic indicator and cyclone class, multiply the population raster by the class mask and sum per admin unit using zonal statistics.

trans-unit.source: **Compute facility exposure:** Sample the cyclone exposure raster at facility point locations, then count and percentage per admin unit per class.
trans-unit.target: **Compute facility exposure:** Sample the cyclone exposure raster at facility point locations, then count and percentage per admin unit per class.

trans-unit.source: Population exposed per demographic group per class: `kt34_total_pop_cat1`, `kt34_female_pop_cat2`, `kt34_children_u5_cat3`, etc.
trans-unit.target: Population exposed per demographic group per class: `kt34_total_pop_cat1`, `kt34_female_pop_cat2`, `kt34_children_u5_cat3`, etc.

trans-unit.source: Facility counts and percentages per class: `kt34_education_count_cat1`, `kt34_education_perc_cat1`, etc.
trans-unit.target: Facility counts and percentages per class: `kt34_education_count_cat1`, `kt34_education_perc_cat1`, etc.

trans-unit.source: Estimates the travel time (in minutes) from at-risk areas (flooded or cyclone-affected population) to the nearest safe zone using least-cost path analysis on a motorized friction surface. This provides a measure of how quickly people in hazard-prone areas can reach areas not affected by the hazard.
trans-unit.target: Estimates the travel time (in minutes) from at-risk areas (flooded or cyclone-affected population) to the nearest safe zone using least-cost path analysis on a motorized friction surface. This provides a measure of how quickly people in hazard-prone areas can reach areas not affected by the hazard.

trans-unit.source: **Flood Hazard Rasters:** GLOFAS flood depth rasters for each return period (RP), produced by the *Flood Exposure* asset.
trans-unit.target: **Flood Hazard Rasters:** GLOFAS flood depth rasters for each return period (RP), produced by the *Flood Exposure* asset.

trans-unit.source: **Cyclone Exposure Raster:** IBTrACS cyclone exposure raster (binary: affected / not affected), produced by the *Cyclone Exposure* asset.
trans-unit.target: **Cyclone Exposure Raster:** IBTrACS cyclone exposure raster (binary: affected / not affected), produced by the *Cyclone Exposure* asset.

trans-unit.source: **Friction Surface:** Global motorized friction surface COG (2020) hosted on MinIO (`2020_motorized_friction_surface_cog.tif`), representing travel impedance per pixel.
trans-unit.target: **Friction Surface:** Global motorized friction surface COG (2020) hosted on MinIO (`2020_motorized_friction_surface_cog.tif`), representing travel impedance per pixel.

trans-unit.source: **WorldPop Population Rasters:** Used to identify the demographic composition of at-risk areas.
trans-unit.target: **WorldPop Population Rasters:** Used to identify the demographic composition of at-risk areas.

trans-unit.source: **Administrative Boundaries** (GeoJSON): Used to aggregate travel time statistics per admin unit.
trans-unit.target: **Administrative Boundaries** (GeoJSON): Used to aggregate travel time statistics per admin unit.

trans-unit.source: Mean travel time from flooded areas to safe zone (for return period `{rp}`)
trans-unit.target: Mean travel time from flooded areas to safe zone (for return period `{rp}`)

trans-unit.source: Maximum travel time from flooded areas to safe zone
trans-unit.target: Maximum travel time from flooded areas to safe zone

trans-unit.source: Median travel time from flooded areas to safe zone
trans-unit.target: Median travel time from flooded areas to safe zone

trans-unit.source: Number of flooded pixels with computed travel time
trans-unit.target: Number of flooded pixels with computed travel time

trans-unit.source: Mean travel time from cyclone-affected areas to safe zone
trans-unit.target: Mean travel time from cyclone-affected areas to safe zone

trans-unit.source: Maximum travel time from cyclone-affected areas to safe zone
trans-unit.target: Maximum travel time from cyclone-affected areas to safe zone

trans-unit.source: Median travel time from cyclone-affected areas to safe zone
trans-unit.target: Median travel time from cyclone-affected areas to safe zone

trans-unit.source: Number of cyclone-affected pixels with computed travel time
trans-unit.target: Number of cyclone-affected pixels with computed travel time

trans-unit.source: **Load hazard raster** (flood depth per RP or cyclone binary raster) for the country.
trans-unit.target: **Load hazard raster** (flood depth per RP or cyclone binary raster) for the country.

trans-unit.source: **Fetch friction surface** window covering the same extent and align to the hazard raster CRS/resolution.
trans-unit.target: **Fetch friction surface** window covering the same extent and align to the hazard raster CRS/resolution.

trans-unit.source: **Safe zones:** pixels where hazard ≤ threshold (flood) or hazard = 0 (cyclone).
trans-unit.target: **Safe zones:** pixels where hazard ≤ threshold (flood) or hazard = 0 (cyclone).

trans-unit.source: **Downsample** if pixel count exceeds performance limit (10 million pixels).
trans-unit.target: **Downsample** if pixel count exceeds performance limit (10 million pixels).

trans-unit.source: **Compute least-cost distances** using `MCP_Geometric` from scikit-image, treating safe zones as source cells and friction as cost per meter.
trans-unit.target: **Compute least-cost distances** using `MCP_Geometric` from scikit-image, treating safe zones as source cells and friction as cost per meter.

trans-unit.source: **Upsample** travel time raster back to original resolution if downsampled.
trans-unit.target: **Upsample** travel time raster back to original resolution if downsampled.

trans-unit.source: **Summarize per admin unit:** mean, max, median travel time and pixel count for at-risk zones.
trans-unit.target: **Summarize per admin unit:** mean, max, median travel time and pixel count for at-risk zones.

trans-unit.source: **Write CSV** with flood evacuability per RP and cyclone evacuability columns.
trans-unit.target: **Write CSV** with flood evacuability per RP and cyclone evacuability columns.
